# Feature: Auth-aware crawl with storage-state

<!-- auto-generated by /implement on 2026-07-17 — do not edit manually unless correcting intent -->

## Intent
Dogfood: `--runtime` + storage-state did not yield useful authenticated multi-step crawl flows. Pass Playwright storageState into `--crawl` / `--auto` crawl so protected routes can be visited with a session.

## Preconditions
- Interactive crawl and runtime storageState support exist

## Happy Path
- [ ] `crawlUncoveredRoutes` accepts `storageStatePath` and creates an authenticated browser context
- [ ] Scan passes `--storage-state` / config into crawl
- [ ] Integration test with fixture cookie / storage-state reaches a protected route and records ≥2 steps when safe clickables exist
- [ ] Missing storageState path warns and continues without session
- [ ] No browo-specific hardcodes

## Edge Cases
- [ ] Destructive actions still gated by safeMode
- [ ] Playwright unavailable → empty result (existing)

## Regression
- [ ] Unauthenticated crawl still works
- [ ] Runtime storageState path unchanged

## Assumptions
- Reuse same storageState JSON format as runtime capture

## Screenshots
| Step | Filename |
|------|----------|
| n/a | CLI/Playwright |

## Implementation Notes
- `CrawlOptions.storageStatePath` → authenticated `browser.newContext`
- CLI scan passes `--storage-state` / config into crawl
- Tests: `crawl.auth.test.ts` fixture with cookie-gated `/app`
